concerns.text: T033 implements McNemar's test for pass@1, but spec.md FR-005 explicitly requires paired Wilcoxon signed-rank test on pass@1 scores. This is a semantic weakening of the statistical methodology requirement without spec amendment.
T035 implements Bonferroni correction for 3 hypotheses, but spec.md FR-005 specifies correction for TWO hypotheses (accuracy and latency only). Token reduction is not a gating hypothesis per spec.
T036 implements rank-biserial correlation for effect sizes, but spec.md FR-006 explicitly requires Cohen's d. This is a constraint violation on the effect size metric.
T003 documents power analysis for n=164, but spec.md FR-010 requires sample size ≥200 problems at power ≥0.8. The task does not address this constraint mismatch.
No task implements random seed pinning in code/, violating constitution.md Principle I (Reproducibility). This is a constitutional constraint that must be preserved in tasks.
No task generates or records dataset checksums under data/, violating constitution.md Principle III (Data Hygiene). T001 only records version in README.md, not checksums.
No task implements Reference-Validator Agent verification gate for external citations, violating constitution.md Principle II (Verified Accuracy). This is a constitutional constraint.

SC-003 in spec.md states accuracy/latency analysis proceeds ONLY IF token reduction is achieved (gating), but tasks follow plan.md which makes it descriptive (non-gating). This is a silent constraint relaxation.
SC-005 in spec.md states experiment is 'under-sampled' if drop rate >5%, but tasks follow plan.md which allows proceeding with documentation. This weakens the requirement without amendment.
